name: Approach
intuition_and_algorithm:
The problem is to count the number of land cells that cannot reach the boundary of the grid.
                            If a land cell is connected (directly or indirectly) to the boundary, then it is not an
                            enclave.
                            So, the strategy is:
                        
First, identify all boundary land cells.
Mark all land cells connected to them using BFS.
The remaining unvisited land cells are enclaves.


Approach


Traverse the boundary of the grid (first row, last row, first col, last col).
Whenever a boundary cell is land (1), run BFS from it and mark all reachable land cells
                                as visited.
After BFS traversal, iterate over the entire grid and count the number of land cells
                                which are still unvisited â these are enclaves.

complexities.time:
O(N Ã M), Each cell is visited at most once in BFS/DFS
                        traversal.
complexities.space:
O(N Ã M), Visited array and BFS queue may store all cells in
                        the worst case.
